title: ranunculus bulbosus subsp. gallecicus
intro: Ranunculus bulbosus subsp. gallecicus, also known as Gallaecian buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the family Ranunculaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, making it a potential treatment for conditions like bronchitis and pneumonia. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus bulbosus subsp. gallecicus include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, although its usage and efficacy should be consulted with a medical professional before consumption.
